Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Excusing Plaintiff From Obtaining Defaults And Default Judgments Against Defendants
Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

PLAINTIFF BING FU KUNG'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER EXCUSING PLAINTIFF FROM OBTAINING DEFAULTS AND DEFAULT JUDGMENTS AGAINST DEFENDANTS.

The court treats plaintiff Kung's motion for order excusing plaintiff from obtaining defaults against defendants as a motion to stay and GRANTS the motion to stay. The instant derivative action is stayed until the direct action (Bing Fu Kung v. Christopher Carthy, et al. (CGC-18-565765) is resolved or further order of the court. Litigating the direct action will likely resolve most, if not all, of the issues in this derivative lawsuit. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/RBU)
